## Structure

TCP HandshakeStart — client's first packet on a fresh TCP
connection. Fixed 3-byte body `80 00 78`.

```
[0..1]   80 00                  TCP opcode (constant)
[2]      0x78                   handshake-start constant ('x' ASCII)
```

All 71 retail observations are byte-identical: `80 00 78`. NO
variation.

## Variants

Single 3-byte form. Pure constant.

## Observed contexts

Client-to-server TCP handshake initiation. Fired immediately
after TCP-connect to signal "ready for auth flow". The server
responds with HandshakeA (`80 01 66`).

Full TCP handshake exchange:
```
C→S 0x8000 0x78  (this packet)
S→C 0x8001 0x66  (HandshakeA)
C→S 0x8002 ?     (HandshakeB)
S→C 0x8003 0x68  (HandshakeC)
```

Same 3-byte form on patch + info server connections.

## Open questions

- Why specifically `0x78` ('x' ASCII)? Probably a hardcoded NC2
  protocol-version constant matching HandshakeA's `0x66` and
  HandshakeC's `0x68`. The byte values look like an alphabet
  sequence (f, x, h?) — magic constants.

## Server-side handler

Decoded by the gameserver TCP listener — recognises the 3-byte
handshake-start and triggers the HandshakeA reply.
